summary: Cleaned up sysdolphin baselib debug code by replacing ad-hoc externs and linker force-active hacks with clearer declarations and layout modeling. The PR moved HSD_Synth function-pointer externs into synth.h, included debug.h/synth.h from debug.c, modeled the debug OSContext plus adjacent 0x10-byte unknown storage as a DebugContext struct, used the string literal "" directly instead of a one-byte static empty string, replaced a stripped dummy asm function with `#pragma peephole off`, and removed the ldscript FORCEACTIVE workaround for HSD_Debug_804C28D0. It also normalized a stack-size constant from decimal 65536 to hex 0x10000.
what_changed: In `debug.c`, local extern declarations for `HSD_Synth_804D7710`, `HSD_Synth_804D7714`, and `HSD_Synth_804D7718` were removed and replaced by including `synth.h`.
In `synth.h`, the HSD_Synth function-pointer extern declarations were added with a Dolphin OS include so `OSContext*` is available.

In `debug.c`, `OSContext HSD_Debug_804C2608` plus separate unknown storage `char HSD_Debug_804C28D0[0x10]` were replaced by a single `struct DebugContext { OSContext context; u8 unk[0x10]; } HSD_Debug_804C2608;`.
Calls to `OSSaveContext` and `HSD_Synth_804D7714` were updated to pass `&HSD_Debug_804C2608.context` instead of the old bare `OSContext` object address.
Removed `HSD_Debug_804D6010`, a static one-byte empty string, and changed `__assert` to call `HSD_Panic(str, arg1, "")` directly.
Removed the commented MUST_MATCH force-active workaround and dummy stripped asm function from `debug.c`; under `MUST_MATCH`, the file now uses `#pragma peephole off`.
Removed the `FORCEACTIVE { HSD_Debug_804C28D0 }` block and associated comment from `ldscript.lcf`.
Changed `_stack_addr` expression in `ldscript.lcf` from decimal `65536` to hex `0x10000`, preserving the value while matching surrounding style.
smart_moves: Moved cross-file synth globals into `synth.h` instead of keeping private externs in `debug.c`, making the dependency explicit and reusable.

Modeled the adjacent unknown 0x10-byte area as part of a larger debug context object, avoiding a linker-script crowbar for a separate symbol and making the save-context calls type-accurate through `.context`.
Replaced a synthetic stripped asm function with the more direct `#pragma peephole off`, reducing unexplained matching scaffolding.
Used the empty string literal directly instead of preserving a dedicated static object where the diff indicates it was unnecessary.
Removed obsolete and profanity-laden workaround comments from source and linker script, improving maintainability without adding unsupported speculation.
decomp_lessons: If an apparently separate unknown symbol is only needed to shape object layout, consider whether it is actually trailing storage within a larger struct; modeling that can remove force-active/linker hacks.
Prefer shared header declarations for globals used across baselib modules over repeated local `extern` declarations in `.c` files.
For matching older CodeWarrior behavior, a targeted pragma such as `#pragma peephole off` can be cleaner than inserting dummy stripped asm solely to influence codegen.
When a forced-active linker-script entry exists only to keep an otherwise artificial symbol alive, revisit the data model; a better struct layout may eliminate the need.
Use style-consistent numeric literals in linker scripts; `0x10000` communicates alignment/size intent better than decimal `65536` in this context.
naming_conventions: Unmatched/unknown runtime symbols retain address-based names such as `HSD_Debug_804C2608` and `HSD_Synth_804D7714`.
A local layout helper was named descriptively as `struct DebugContext` with a typed `context` member and an unknown byte tail named `unk`.
Baselib include style in this cleanup uses local quoted includes for sibling project headers: `#include "debug.h"` and `#include "synth.h"`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a MUST_MATCH-only stripped asm function containing four `nop` instructions that had been used to affect early CodeWarrior peephole behavior.
Replaced the dummy asm matching scaffold with `#pragma peephole off` under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`.
Removed an ldscript `FORCEACTIVE` block for `HSD_Debug_804C28D0`, suggesting the new object layout no longer needed that symbol-preservation workaround.
Changed the debug context symbol so OS functions receive `&HSD_Debug_804C2608.context`, preserving the expected `OSContext*` interface while allowing extra trailing bytes in the object.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that further rename or type `HSD_Debug_804C2608` / `DebugContext`.
Search for other local HSD_Synth extern declarations that should include `synth.h` instead.
Audit remaining ldscript `FORCEACTIVE` workarounds to see whether any can be replaced by better struct/object modeling.
Search for other dummy stripped asm functions used only to alter CodeWarrior peephole behavior and consider `#pragma peephole off` where evidence supports it.
